reason: Both answers correctly identify alert noise as the primary problem, correctly conclude the 6-person weekly rotation is fine and shouldn't be touched first, and give similar concrete steps (audit alerts, route non-urgent to business hours, ongoing review). Neither explicitly mentions reduced sprint commitment during on-call weeks, a point both miss equally. B is slightly more concise and adds a couple of sharper practical mechanisms (an explicit page-per-shift budget that triggers review, and muting noisy alerts by default until fixed, folded into handoff process), while A includes a somewhat unsupported/filler stat ('most teams find 70-80% of pages can be eliminated') that doesn't add real value. Overall very comparable, with B slightly tighter and marginally more actionable.
